title: Ensure all data in BigQuery has been classified

input_row.title: Ensure all data in BigQuery has been classified
input_row.description: BigQuery tables can contain sensitive data that for security purposes should be discovered, monitored, classified, and protected. Google Cloud's Sensitive Data Protection tools can automatically provide data classification of all BigQuery data across an organization.
input_row.rationale: Using a cloud service or 3rd party software to continuously monitor and automate the process of data discovery and classification for BigQuery tables is an important part of protecting the data. Sensitive Data Protection is a fully managed data protection and data privacy platform that uses machine learning and pattern matching to discover and classify sensitive data in Google Cloud.
input_row.audit: 1. Go to Cloud DLP by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/dlp/landing/dataProfiles/configurations. 2. Verify there is a discovery scan configuration either for the organization or project.
input_row.remediation: Enable profiling: 1. Go to Cloud DLP by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/dlp/landing/dataProfiles/configurations 2. Click "Create Configuration" 3. For projects follow https://cloud.google.com/dlp/docs/profile-project. For organizations or folders follow https://cloud.google.com/dlp/docs/profile-org-folder Review findings: • Columns or tables with high data risk have evidence of sensitive information without additional protections. To lower the data risk score, consider doing the following: • For columns containing sensitive data, apply a BigQuery policy tag to restrict access to accounts with specific access rights. • De-identify the raw sensitive data using de-identification techniques like masking and tokenization. Incorporate findings into your security and governance operations: • Enable sending findings into your security and posture services. You can publish data profiles to Security Command Center and Chronicle. • Automate remediation or enable alerting of new or changed data risk with Pub/Sub.
input_row.impact: There is a cost associated with using Sensitive Data Protection. There is also typically a cost associated with 3rd party tools that perform similar processes and protection.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. Navigate to the Google Cloud Console and click on the "Data Loss Prevention" (DLP) section. Use the following link: [Cloud DLP Landing Page](https://console.cloud.google.com/dlp/landing/dataProfiles/configurations).
2. In the DLP section, look for "Configuration" and verify whether there is a discovery scan configuration set either at the organization or project level.
3. Validate that at least one configuration for data profiling exists. This indicates that data classification processes are in place for the BigQuery tables.
4. If no configuration exists, you need to proceed to create a configuration by clicking on "Create Configuration".
5. For projects, follow the guidance in the documentation at [Profile Project](https://cloud.google.com/dlp/docs/profile-project). For organizations or folders, refer to [Profile Org/Folder](https://cloud.google.com/dlp/docs/profile-org-folder).
6. After creating the configuration, review the findings that may highlight columns or tables with sensitive data risks, and check if appropriate actions have been taken to secure that sensitive data.
7. Confirm that sensitive data columns have policy tags applied, and check if de-identification techniques have been utilized for data protection.
8. Validate that findings from the scans are being integrated into security operations, such as sending data profiles to the Security Command Center or enabling alerting via Pub/Sub for newly identified risks.

**EXPECTED SECURE CONFIGURATION:**
- A valid discovery scan configuration should be created and enabled at either the organization or project level.
- All expected findings related to sensitive data should have appropriate remediation steps applied (such as tagging and de-identification).

**HOW TO CONFIRM COMPLIANCE:**
- Ensure the presence of active scanning configurations in the Cloud DLP section and review the sensitivity findings for absent or misconfigured policies related to sensitive data in BigQuery.
- Confirm that direct remediation actions are taken based on audit findings, reflecting a proactive approach to data security and compliance per the CIS benchmark.

By completing these steps, you can ensure that compliance with this control is adequately verified.
